seoTitle: Match Bank Transactions in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: How to approve auto-matched bank transactions, run manual searches, and match multiple invoices to a single bank deposit in QuickBooks.
intro: Keeping your books clean means avoiding duplicate entries, and QuickBooks Online gives you three ways to match bank feed transactions to existing records: approving automatic suggestions, running manual searches, and linking several invoices to one large deposit. This guide covers all three.

steps.heading: Open the bank feed
steps.body: Go to All Apps, then Bank Transactions, and select your target account to view the For Review queue.

steps.heading: Approve auto-matches
steps.body: Look for green Match Found labels, review the suggested record details, then click Match to link them without creating duplicates.

steps.heading: Search manually for missing records
steps.body: On an unmatched row, click Find Other Matches and use the filter panel to search by date or exact amount to surface hidden transactions.

steps.heading: Match multiple invoices to one deposit
steps.body: Toggle Match Multiple, check off several open customer payments whose combined total equals the bank deposit amount.

steps.heading: Save and audit
steps.body: Click Save to complete the multi-transaction match, then check the Categorized tab to verify or undo any misaligned matches.

faq.question: What does a green Match Found label mean?
faq.answer: It means QuickBooks has identified a likely matching record already in your books, and clicking Match confirms the link without creating a duplicate.

faq.question: What if QuickBooks can't find an automatic match?
faq.answer: Use Find Other Matches to open manual search filters and locate the transaction by date or exact dollar amount.

faq.question: How do I handle a bank deposit that covers several customer payments?
faq.answer: Use the Match Multiple toggle to select several open invoices whose combined total matches the single bank deposit line.

faq.question: How can I check for mistakes after matching transactions?
faq.answer: Review the Categorized tab, which lets you audit completed matches and undo anything that looks misaligned.
